provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Preventive Detention (Communication With Detainees) Regulations.

In these Regulations— " Act " means the Preventive Detention Act 1 ; 1 Cap. 361 " detainee " means a person detained under an order made under the Act ; " Minister " means the Minister responsible for home affairs.
No detainee shall, except with the previous authority in writing of the Minister , receive any visitor or write or receive any letter or other written communication.

The provisions of section 89 (which relates to visits to sick prisoners), section 46 (which relates to communications with prisoners), and section 44 (which relates to visits by ministers of religion) of the Prisons Act 2 , and of paragraph (5) of rule 13 of the Prisons Rules (which relate to the receipt of visits and the writing and receipt of letters by civil prisoners) shall not apply to any detainee . 2 Cap. 58 History of this document 31 July 2002 this version Consolidation Related documents More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all. Free Access to the law supports the rule of law and access to justice. TanzLII partners with African Legal Information Institute and Laws.Africa , who provide it with technical support.
